questions.text: According to Werner's theory, the primary valency of a central metal atom is:
options: Ionizable and corresponds to oxidation state
questions.explanation: Primary valency is equivalent to the oxidation state and is ionizable, whereas secondary valency is non-ionizable.

questions.text: A ligand that can coordinate to a metal through two different atoms is called a/an:
questions.explanation: Ambidentate ligands like SCN⁻ or NO₂⁻ have two different donor atoms but coordinate through only one at a time.

questions.text: The coordination number of 'Fe' in [Fe(C₂O₄)₃]³⁻ is:
questions.explanation: Oxalate (C₂O₄²⁻) is a didentate ligand. 3 ligands x 2 donor sites = 6.

questions.text: What is the IUPAC name of [Pt(NH₃)₂Cl(NO₂)]?
questions.explanation: Ligands are named alphabetically (ammine, chlorido, nitrito) followed by the metal and its oxidation state.

questions.text: Which of the following ligands shows the 'Chelate effect'?
questions.explanation: Polydentate ligands form ring structures with the metal, which are significantly more stable than unidentate complexes.

questions.text: EDTA is a ________ ligand.
questions.explanation: Ethylene diamine tetraacetate has six donor atoms (two N and four O).

questions.text: The type of isomerism shown by [Co(NH₃)₅(SO₄)]Br and [Co(NH₃)₅Br]SO₄ is:
questions.explanation: These isomers produce different ions in solution (Br⁻ vs SO₄²⁻).

questions.text: Geometrical isomerism (cis-trans) is NOT possible in which geometry?
questions.explanation: In tetrahedral geometry, all positions are adjacent to each other, so no cis-trans variation exists.

questions.text: Facial (fac) and meridional (mer) isomerism is associated with which type of complex?
questions.explanation: This specific geometrical isomerism occurs in octahedral complexes with three ligands of one type and three of another.

questions.text: According to Valence Bond Theory (VBT), a complex with sp³d² hybridization has ________ geometry.
questions.explanation: sp³d² uses the outer 'd' orbitals, resulting in high-spin outer orbital complexes.

questions.text: The complex [Ni(CN)₄]²⁻ is diamagnetic and has dsp² hybridization. Its shape is:
questions.explanation: dsp² hybridization always corresponds to square planar geometry.

questions.text: Crystal Field Theory (CFT) considers the metal-ligand bond to be:
questions.explanation: CFT assumes ligands are point charges and the interaction is purely electrostatic.

questions.text: In an octahedral crystal field, the d-orbitals split into two sets called:
questions.explanation: t₂g (lower energy: dxy, dyz, dzx) and eg (higher energy: dx²-y², dz²).

questions.text: Which of the following is a strong field ligand according to the spectrochemical series?
questions.explanation: CN⁻ and CO are at the high end of the series, causing large crystal field splitting.

questions.text: For a d⁴ ion in an octahedral field with a strong ligand (Δo > P), the configuration is:
questions.explanation: Since splitting energy is higher than pairing energy, the fourth electron pairs up in the t₂g level.

questions.text: The color of coordination compounds is usually due to:
questions.explanation: Electronic transitions between the split d-orbital levels absorb specific wavelengths of visible light.

questions.text: What happens to the color of [Ti(H₂O)₆]Cl₃ when it is heated?
questions.explanation: Heating removes the water ligands; without the crystal field splitting from ligands, d-d transitions stop and color is lost.

questions.text: Which complex is used in the treatment of cancer?
questions.explanation: cis-[Pt(NH₃)₂Cl₂] is a widely used chemotherapy drug.

questions.text: Chlorophyll contains which metal ion?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is a coordination compound of magnesium.

questions.text: Vitamin B12 is a coordination complex of:
questions.explanation: Vitamin B12 (cyanocobalamin) contains cobalt.

questions.text: The IUPAC name of [Ni(CO)₄] is:
questions.explanation: Carbonyl is a neutral ligand, and the metal is named normally with its zero oxidation state.

questions.text: In the complex K₄[Fe(CN)₆], the 'counter ion' is:
questions.explanation: Counter ions are the ionizable groups outside the coordination sphere.

questions.text: Which of the following is a homoleptic complex?
questions.explanation: Homoleptic complexes have only one type of ligand (all four are CN⁻ in this case).

questions.text: The crystal field splitting energy for tetrahedral complexes (Δt) is related to octahedral (Δo) as:
questions.explanation: Tetrahedral splitting is always smaller than octahedral splitting.

questions.text: Which of the following shows linkage isomerism?
questions.explanation: Ambidentate ligands like NO₂⁻ can link via N (nitro) or O (nitrito).

questions.text: A complex with coordination number 4 can have which geometries?
questions.explanation: Depending on hybridization (sp³ or dsp²), it can be tetrahedral or square planar.

questions.text: In naming anionic complexes, the metal name ends with:
questions.explanation: For example, iron becomes ferrate, cobalt becomes cobaltate.

questions.text: Optical isomerism is most common in which type of coordination compounds?
questions.explanation: Octahedral complexes with chelating ligands (like [Co(en)₃]³⁺) often lack a plane of symmetry.

questions.text: Synergic bonding involves:
options: Both sigma donation and pi back-donation
questions.explanation: Typical in metal carbonyls, where CO donates electrons and metal back-donates to CO pi-antibonding orbitals.

questions.text: Which complex is used to remove lead poisoning from the body?
questions.explanation: EDTA forms a stable complex with lead which is then excreted from the body.

questions.text: The magnetic moment of [MnCl₆]³⁻ (high spin) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Mn³⁺ is d⁴. High spin means 4 unpaired electrons. μ = √(4(6)) = √24 ≈ 4.90 BM.

questions.text: A heteroleptic complex is one where:
options: There is more than one type of ligand
questions.explanation: Heteroleptic refers to 'different' ligands.

questions.text: How many moles of AgCl will be precipitated when excess AgNO₃ is added to 1 mole of [Co(NH₃)₄Cl₂]Cl?
questions.explanation: Only the chloride ion outside the coordination bracket is ionizable.

questions.text: The d-orbital used in dsp² hybridization is:
questions.explanation: The dx²-y² orbital lies in the plane of the ligands in square planar geometry.

questions.text: Haemoglobin is a coordination compound of:
questions.explanation: Haemoglobin is the red pigment of blood containing iron(II) porphyrin complex.

questions.text: Which of the following complexes will have the highest molar conductivity?
questions.explanation: Option 1 produces 4 ions per molecule, which is more than any other option.

questions.text: What is the coordination number of 'Ca' in [Ca(EDTA)]²⁻?
questions.explanation: Since EDTA is hexadentate, the coordination number is 6.

questions.text: Transition metal carbonyls are stable due to:
questions.explanation: The M-C bond is strengthened by the simultaneous sigma and pi interactions.

questions.text: The spectrochemical series arranges ligands in order of their:
questions.explanation: It determines whether a complex will be high-spin or low-spin.

questions.text: Octahedral complexes of the type [Ma₂b₂c₂] can show:
questions.explanation: They can have multiple geometrical forms, some of which are chiral.
